props.title: Dunnhumby | Work

ogImage.alt: Laptop on a round white table displaying a website, next to a stack of books and a coffee cup in a cozy living room setting.

props.description: How we ran an in-depth discovery with dunnhumby’s stakeholder teams, to understand their needs, and establish the foundations for a future design system.

image.alt: Laptop on a round white table displaying a website, next to a stack of books and a coffee cup in a cozy living room setting.

props.title: dunnhumby
props.content:
## Need

When we started talking to dunnhumby about UI and the evolution of their product ecosystem, they presented us with a question: how can we establish foundational requirements for a future design system?

We kicked off an in-depth discovery to gain a full understanding of the client’s historical context and challenges. This meant we’d be in the position to make the most useful recommendations possible to help keep the client on a successful path.


## Action

We need to collaborate closely with dunnhumby’s stakeholder to arrive at the most useful recommendations. This meant:

- Analysing existing component libraries, brand guidelines, and user interfaces
- Documenting tech stacks
- Evaluating Design and DesignOps tools, such as Figma and zeroheight
- Assessing release strategies and design-engineering handoffs

Our findings were synthesised into a comprehensive report, which we refined and presented regularly as new insights emerged.


## Outcome

The report was successful in:

- Articulating the current state of dunnhumby’s UI
- Making recommendations for managing the existing component library
- Providing strategic guidance for the direction of future design system

This enabled UI and product efforts to accelerate, helping them quickly progress to implementing next steps and higher-level recommendations.

Our involvement progressed into:

- Developing the foundations for a new design system
- Highlighting product implications
- Helping establish the new internal design system team
props.standfirst: Laying the strategic foundations of a future design system.
